title: Commentary on Membership
content:
As of December 31, 2026, the credit union reported 44 current members as of that date.
Membership levels remained flat at 0% from the previous year end and increased by 2% on a quarter-over-quarter basis. Potential membership totaled 156, remaining unchanged.

Operational efficiency metrics remain stable. Members per full-time employee have shown variability, indicating that staffing levels show some misalignment with current service demand.

Shares per member rose during the period. This increase means existing members are holding a larger average deposit balance than before, which strengthens the funding base even as membership count held flat year-over-year; total growth will now depend more on deepening relationships and increasing balances among these steady accounts rather than acquiring new ones.

Overall, while membership remained stable with modest quarterly gains, rising shares per member signals that existing members are holding larger average deposits. Continued focus on deposit retention and relationship management remains important to capitalize on this deeper funding base.
